jokes.text: I was in the middle of bidding on a bag of bras on eBay.
jokes.setup: Liz is interrupted from something presumably important
jokes.punchline: It's bidding on a bag of bras on eBay - absurdly specific and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - specific, pathetic, and reveals Liz's sad personal life in one efficient line

jokes.text: It's not, hon. I saw that in a movie once, but in the movie, the guy was dead.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if meeting the boss is a good thing
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to death from movies as business advice
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of dark humor, efficient structure

jokes.text: I was sitting in that chair a minute ago. Nope. That was me. What can I say? I smell like leather.
jokes.setup: Jack asks how long someone has been there
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation about smelling like leather causing confusion
jokes.explanation: Absurdist character moment with memorable tagline about smelling like leather

jokes.text: Well, I'll be Bake McBrided.
jokes.setup: Standard exclamation setup
jokes.punchline: Made-up folksy expression that sounds authentic but is nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect nonsense folksy expression that sounds real but isn't - very 30 Rock style wordplay

jokes.text: Without Tracy, your show is like my cholesterol. The numbers are killing me.
jokes.setup: Comparison setup about the show being like cholesterol
jokes.punchline: The numbers are killing me - works for both cholesterol and ratings
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning that works perfectly for both sides of the comparison

jokes.text: Got on Wikipedia this week.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions they're getting good media buzz
jokes.punchline: Getting on Wikipedia presented as major media achievement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how pathetic TGS is by treating Wikipedia as major media

jokes.text: A show that is number one in its time slot among men 18 to 49 months left in prison?
jokes.setup: Standard demographic brag about being number one
jokes.punchline: The demographic is prisoners with months left in their sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that starts as standard TV demographic brag then reveals absurd prison demographic

jokes.text: I thought this company was a family, but I guess it's that Austrian family and I am the girl in the basement, and you are the dad who has been brutally--
jokes.setup: Standard corporate family metaphor
jokes.punchline: References specific Austrian incest case before being cut off
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark escalation of corporate family metaphor to specific real-world horror case

jokes.text: Just like the army doctor said to me after my weekend in Okinawa.
jokes.setup: Hank says 'you get one more shot'
jokes.punchline: References mysterious military medical incident
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with mysterious dark backstory implication

jokes.text: Jack Welch once smacked a pretzel out of my hand at the Super Bowl.
jokes.setup: Explaining why GE people are mean
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and petty example of meanness from famous CEO
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd specificity - pretzel slapping is both petty and memorable

jokes.text: Lemon-style. [Everyone Dance Now music plays]
jokes.setup: Liz says she'll do whatever it takes
jokes.punchline: Her personal style apparently involves random dance music
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Liz's random quirks, though somewhat cheap music cue joke

jokes.text: You gonna get me another sandwich or I'm gonna cut your face up so bad you'll have a chin. I will waste you!
jokes.setup: Tracy threatening someone
jokes.punchline: Threat is to cut face so bad they'll have A chin (not multiple)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy nonsense - threatens violence but the logic is completely broken

jokes.text: And I don't really think it's fair for me to be on a jury because I'm a hologram.
jokes.setup: Tracy apparently in a TV show or movie scene
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that he's a hologram as jury excuse
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism - treats being a hologram as mundane jury duty excuse

jokes.text: He told me he feels like a Bartram's Scrub-Hairstreak trying to crawl back into its silky cocoon. Which is a butterfly metaphor, by the way, and not, as I first thought, a list of African-American hair products.
jokes.setup: Liz explains Tracy's butterfly metaphor
jokes.punchline: She initially thought it was hair products due to the name
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection based on how the butterfly name sounds like hair product names

jokes.text: Jesus was black!
jokes.setup: Tracy being prepped for morning show interviews
jokes.punchline: His immediate response to any question is controversial statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - inappropriate response but economical and in character

jokes.text: Really? Is it from that pie place?
jokes.setup: Jack says he has a very full plate
jokes.punchline: Liz takes the metaphor literally and asks about pie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's food obsession and literal thinking

jokes.text: There is no pie.
jokes.setup: Liz's pie question
jokes.punchline: Jack's flat denial with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - economy of words with great timing

jokes.text: In 100 shows, we've done Pam 107 times.
jokes.setup: Congratulating everyone on 100th show
jokes.punchline: They've somehow done one sketch more times than they've done shows
jokes.explanation: Good math joke about their show's repetitiveness and poor planning

jokes.text: Um, that wasn't me. There used to be another guy.
jokes.setup: Someone reminiscing about five years together
jokes.punchline: Danny wasn't there - references cast changes
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about cast changes, though not particularly strong

jokes.text: I will choke you to death with your boyfriend's wig!
jokes.setup: Jenna getting upset about reminiscing
jokes.punchline: Specific threat involving boyfriend's wig
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna threat with specific absurd details about the wig

jokes.text: Can you reminisce about something that's happened since I've been here, 'cause I couldn't see any of that.
jokes.setup: People reminiscing about things before Danny arrived
jokes.punchline: He literally couldn't see things that happened before he was cast
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about cast changes with logical character response

jokes.text: If this turkey goes 100 episodes, I'll have your baby.
jokes.setup: Flashback to first show comment
jokes.punchline: Jenna's over-the-top bet about the show's success
jokes.explanation: Sets up future callback with characteristically dramatic Jenna bet

jokes.text: Wait. I saw that. How? Am I dead?
jokes.setup: Danny seeing a flashback from before he was on the show
jokes.punchline: Questioning if he's dead to explain seeing the past
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about how he could see things from before he was cast

jokes.text: I better lay you across my grandmother's lap in the mating shed.
jokes.setup: Kenneth noting it's been 100 episodes
jokes.punchline: Reference to fulfilling Jenna's baby promise in disturbing Kenneth way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - innocent but deeply disturbing rural reference

jokes.text: The only thing I want latched to my funbags are celebrity DJs.
jokes.setup: Talking about not wanting to be a mother
jokes.punchline: Very specific preference for what should be on her breasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - narcissistic and specific about celebrity preferences

jokes.text: I wish. Albinos get to be watchers in the mating shed.
jokes.setup: Kenneth called an albino goon
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to albinos having special privileges in disturbing rural context
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Kenneth moment - innocent delivery of deeply disturbing content

jokes.text: 'Who wore it best?' I did. I wore it best.
jokes.setup: Imagining being compared to Natalie Portman in magazines
jokes.punchline: Immediate confident assertion she'd win the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - doesn't even consider she might lose

jokes.text: Tonight, TGS will not be the worst thing on television. It'll be John Stossel.
jokes.setup: Inspirational speech about not being the worst
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to John Stossel being worse
jokes.explanation: Good structure with specific celebrity reference for the punchline

jokes.text: Why am I showing you guys this? It's gonna jinx everything. Plus you're a couple of pervs.
jokes.setup: Showing photo of his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Realizes both that it's bad luck and they're perverts
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Isn't that show already half-dead?
jokes.setup: Learning TGS is on the sixth floor
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of the show as already dying
jokes.explanation: Decent but predictable joke about the show's poor reputation

jokes.text: Mystery novels written by janitors?
jokes.setup: Asked what he finds fascinating
jokes.punchline: Very specific and random literary preference
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist specificity that sounds like something Jack would say

jokes.text: See? I got a new hair. It's white and it hurts, but--
jokes.setup: Jack showing he has other things going on
jokes.punchline: Single white hair that causes pain as life accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's vanity and aging anxiety

jokes.text: Other possible side effects include hallucinations, revelation of secrets, telling of truths, flashbacks, headaches, nostalgia.
jokes.setup: Listing gas leak side effects
jokes.punchline: List goes from medical to emotional/narrative effects
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about sitcom plot devices disguised as medical effects

jokes.text: I don't know if there was a party planned--
jokes.setup: Mentioning it's his last day
jokes.punchline: Awkward hope for retirement party that probably wasn't planned
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment but not particularly strong

jokes.text: I refuse to have long, gas-induced conversations with any of you people.
jokes.setup: Jack ordering evacuation
jokes.punchline: Explicitly refusing the episode's main plot device
jokes.explanation: Great meta joke with Jack rejecting the sitcom plot device directly

jokes.text: More like 100 little strokes.
jokes.setup: Comment about 100 shows
jokes.punchline: Dark medical reference about the stress
jokes.explanation: Good dark Pete humor about work stress with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Sure, he was an idiot, but he made great chili, and he didn't care if I watched TV during sex.
jokes.setup: Liz reminiscing about Dennis
jokes.punchline: Her criteria for a good relationship are pathetically low
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment showing her low standards and priorities

jokes.text: Just follow these... Troll penises! Oh, God! What have I done?
jokes.setup: Kenneth leading evacuation before gas affects him
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts hallucinating inappropriate things
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment with innocent character saying inappropriate things

jokes.text: Why don't you shut your mouth, back that ass up and make me a sandwich?
jokes.setup: Tracy on morning show still in character
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate response to talk show host
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy inappropriate behavior though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I didn't go to Africa. I was hiding in a warehouse in Queens, watching vintage pornography.
jokes.setup: Tracy supposed to discuss his Africa trip
jokes.punchline: Admits he never went and was doing something gross instead
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy honesty that makes him look worse, perfect for character

jokes.text: What does that even mean? It's new haircuts, but you can make salads with them.
jokes.setup: Morning show segment about hair make-unders
jokes.punchline: Tracy's confused explanation makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy nonsense - tries to explain something and makes it more confusing

jokes.text: 'Broken keyboard talk show'? I write what I know. Also I broke my keyboard.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning Frank's sketch title
jokes.punchline: He literally wrote about his broken keyboard because his keyboard is broken
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - meta and practical at the same time

jokes.text: And what is Farm Aid? Is it a drink? Is it a drug? Is it a bandage you put on a barn?
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about being contacted by charities
jokes.punchline: Series of absurd guesses about what Farm Aid could be
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy ignorance with escalating absurd guesses

jokes.text: See, that's the kind of lazy stand-up I'll never do again.
jokes.setup: Tracy making observations about Farm Aid
jokes.punchline: He calls his own jokes lazy stand-up while doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta moment where Tracy criticizes the exact joke style he just used

jokes.text: F-u, l.l. Spells 'full,' because you're full of BS, Liz Lemon.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to tell Liz off
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily spells out 'full' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment - tries to be clever with spelling but it's unnecessarily complicated

jokes.text: Be like Michael McDonald and take it to the streets.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Tracy advice
jokes.punchline: Uses Doobie Brothers song title as literal advice
jokes.explanation: Decent reference joke with song title as advice

jokes.text: I think Bono got in my limo.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about his problems
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity invasion problem
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity problem that only Tracy would have

jokes.text: No. And I wasn't even drinking anything.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if Liz thinks she'd be a good mother
jokes.punchline: Immediate 'no' followed by clarification she wasn't impaired
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat with great timing and efficiency

jokes.text: I'm in my 40s, very difficult, and not that good at playing La Realite.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why pregnancy would be good excuse for not working
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest self-assessment with made-up French word
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna self-awareness with pretentious made-up French

jokes.text: Who will grow up to be a little gay fancy man.
jokes.setup: Liz telling Jenna to think about having a child
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption about what Jenna's child would be like
jokes.explanation: Good logical prediction based on Jenna's personality

jokes.text: I'm getting too old for this [sound] that comes from this gas pipe.
jokes.setup: Gas leak guy getting frustrated
jokes.punchline: 'Too old for this shit' reference but can't say 'shit' on TV
jokes.explanation: Clever censorship workaround with famous movie line

jokes.text: I'm Jack Donaghy, CEO of General Electric. Who are you? I'm Jack Donaghy. You were Jack Donaghy.
jokes.setup: Jack meeting alternate version of himself
jokes.punchline: Confusion over who is the 'real' Jack Donaghy
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the alternate Jack storyline

jokes.text: I'll tell you what I don't do. Wear a striped tie with a striped shirt. It's called 'Power Clashing' and I do it because I can.
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack explaining what he does
jokes.punchline: Absurd fashion choice presented as power move
jokes.explanation: Perfect alternate Jack - takes fashion faux pas and makes it about power

jokes.text: We made the cover during meetings history month? I made the cover.
jokes.setup: Discussion about magazine cover
jokes.punchline: Alternate Jack corrects that only he made the cover
jokes.explanation: Good alternate Jack superiority moment

jokes.text: And here... Isn't even GE anymore. It's Kableclown. 'Town,' Donaghy, and that's not funny.
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack criticizing Jack's situation
jokes.punchline: Jack's attempt to make 'Kabletown' funny fails
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's failed attempt at humor

jokes.text: Look at my claws! Sharks don't have claws. You don't even know what a shark is anymore!
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to still be a shark
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's confused about basic shark anatomy
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how far Jack has fallen - doesn't know shark anatomy

jokes.text: Baseball caps with stupid sayings from the show written on them. What the hell is a 'Pwomp'?
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack criticizing Jack's work
jokes.punchline: References show's internal jokes as meaningless
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about how show inside jokes are meaningless to outsiders

jokes.text: It's when two fat people-- I don't care!
jokes.setup: Jack starting to explain 'Pwomp'
jokes.punchline: Alternate Jack cuts him off, showing how pathetic it is
jokes.explanation: Good callback with alternate Jack's disgusted reaction

jokes.text: I am a Jedi!
jokes.setup: Jack's gas-induced breakdown
jokes.punchline: Random Star Wars reference as assertion of power
jokes.explanation: Decent gas-induced nonsense but somewhat cheap pop culture reference

jokes.text: I want to take a picture of him with my old-fashioned camera.
jokes.setup: Photographer wanting to photograph Tracy's heroism
jokes.punchline: Specifies old-fashioned camera for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Decent random character detail but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Don't tell anyone I did this.
jokes.setup: Tracy saving someone
jokes.punchline: Asks for anonymity after good deed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's failed attempt at staying bad

jokes.text: I visited my rescue chimp for his birthday last week, and another chimp got mad and bit my nipple off. He played with it, and then he kissed it, and then he ate it.
jokes.setup: Lutz explaining why he won't take his shirt off
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing details about chimp nipple incident
jokes.explanation: Peak Lutz - horrifyingly specific and escalating story with perfect character integration

jokes.text: I've only seen you use that computer to look at pictures of Nate Berkus.
jokes.setup: Cerie defending not bringing Liz's computer
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Liz's apparent obsession
jokes.explanation: Good character observation with specific celebrity reference

jokes.text: Your wet, yet somehow flaky hand.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about holding Liz's hand metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Disgustingly specific description of her hand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack insult - specific, disgusting, and somehow medically accurate

jokes.text: I became the American auto industry, failing to recognize that you can't fix a Lemon
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he failed
jokes.punchline: Auto industry metaphor with Lemon pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent complex metaphor combining auto industry criticism with Lemon name pun

jokes.text: She threw a birthday party for her TV.
jokes.setup: Pete defending Liz as having nothing going on
jokes.punchline: Specific example of how pathetic her life is
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific example of Liz's pathetic loneliness

jokes.text: I knew you were supposed to push, but I didn't say anything.
jokes.setup: Dennis appearing during gas hallucination
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of letting someone suffer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Is this just to talk, or will you be activating my electric underwear?
jokes.setup: Paul meeting with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to electric underwear fetish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Paul L'Astname moment - bizarre fetish reference delivered casually

jokes.text: I mean, what if we had a child that was prettier than us? We'd have to leave it in a desert.
jokes.setup: Paul explaining why they'd be terrible parents
jokes.punchline: Logical conclusion is abandoning attractive child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark logic from Paul - vanity leading to child abandonment

jokes.text: Right now, it's between 'Frisbeeface' and 'Glock,' gender irrelevant.
jokes.setup: Jenna discussing baby names
jokes.punchline: Terrible name choices that ignore gender
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - absurd name choices that prioritize attention over child's welfare

jokes.text: I want to hogtie you and hide you in the luggage compartment of a Greyhound bus with just a bowl of dirty water.
jokes.setup: Paul expressing love for Jenna
jokes.punchline: Romantic declaration that's actually kidnapping fantasy
jokes.explanation: Peak Paul L'Astname - romantic sentiment expressed through disturbing kidnapping scenario

jokes.text: Get out of here, Joey. I'm recording my voicemail message.
jokes.setup: Dennis's voicemail message playing
jokes.punchline: Reveals someone was interrupting his recording
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis detail showing his unprofessional approach to everything

jokes.text: Good evening, sir. I'm Chris Hansen with Dateline NBC. Can I ask you what you're doing here tonight?
jokes.setup: Dennis at restaurant with Liz
jokes.punchline: To Catch a Predator reference implying Dennis looks suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke playing on Dennis's creepy appearance

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm here to boff some chick named Mary. Hey... threesome?
jokes.setup: Dennis responding to Chris Hansen
jokes.punchline: Makes himself look worse then propositions Hansen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - makes bad situation worse with inappropriate proposition

jokes.text: You were a mistake that I made at a time in my life when I could afford to make mistakes.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her feelings about Dennis
jokes.punchline: Frames him as affordable mistake from better times
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that perfectly captures both Liz's self-awareness and her decline

jokes.text: We didn't give up when America sent us back. And we didn't give up when Ireland then just set us adrift on a log, all right?
jokes.setup: Dennis's family history of not giving up
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd rejections ending with being set adrift on a log
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis family history with perfect escalation to absurd log scenario

jokes.text: And out on the Plaza, a bird landed on my apple. I thought he was gonna eat it, but he just sat there. What's next? A different bird landing on a different apple?
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing his day
jokes.punchline: Innocent observation followed by absurd escalation prediction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence with absurd logical progression

jokes.text: I even called a woman's basketball team 'Nappy-headed hos,' but apparently, I'm allowed to talk like that.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to get people mad at him
jokes.punchline: References Don Imus controversy but notes he's allowed to say it
jokes.explanation: Good reference to real controversy with meta-commentary on racial double standards

jokes.text: I mean, look at March of the Penguins. Who was in that?
jokes.setup: Hank saying you can't make movies without actors
jokes.punchline: Points out successful documentary with no human actors
jokes.explanation: Good logical counterpoint delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: How much is a lap dance? I'm a little light on cash, but I have a PayPal account.
jokes.setup: Liz at strip club
jokes.punchline: Offers digital payment for analog service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz awkwardness mixing modern technology with strip club culture

jokes.text: There are tears falling on her boobies, Liz Lemon.
jokes.setup: Stripper being emotionally affected by Tracy's movie
jokes.punchline: Tracy's crude but poetic observation of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - crude but somehow touching observation of the absurd situation

jokes.text: I know you've been molested. That's how we all got here, but I don't want to hear about it.
jokes.setup: Stripper telling emotional story
jokes.punchline: Liz's dark assumption and immediate boundary setting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Liz moment - compassionate but brutally practical

jokes.text: But I wasn't even supposed to say that. The line was, 'Shereen, I hope Dr. Mogutu has good news about my endoscopy,' but I couldn't get it right, so they told me to improv.
jokes.setup: Tracy's inspirational movie quote
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was accidental improv from failed medical dialogue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy deflation - his inspirational moment was just failed line reading

jokes.text: It's not rape if neither party really wants it.
jokes.setup: Jenna planning to seduce Kenneth while he sleeps
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of consent
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna humor with completely twisted logic about consent

jokes.text: I borrowed your whale semen candle. It didn't work, by the way.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking to Kenneth after failed seduction
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of borrowing disgusting candle
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd detail delivered casually - whale semen candle as seduction aid

jokes.text: That's crazy. A man named 'Elia.' That's a giraffe's name.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Elia Kazan
jokes.punchline: Tracy fixates on the name sounding like animal name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy distraction - ignores serious point to focus on name sounding like giraffe

jokes.text: I guess it would take a pretty big gas leak to make you think that was a good idea.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting Tracy shoot someone
jokes.punchline: Tracy notes how crazy the idea is, referencing the episode's premise
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke with Tracy being the voice of reason about the episode's absurdity

jokes.text: Gas has no effect on me at all. When I was growing up, my school, Gerry Cooney Elementary, it was right next to a gas works in Queens.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why gas doesn't affect him
jokes.punchline: Childhood gas exposure at school with boxer's name
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis backstory with specific details about Queens childhood

jokes.text: Our parents voted to spend it on a boat that the families could share, but then that sank.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining gas settlement money
jokes.punchline: Community boat purchase that immediately failed
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis family incompetence with logical progression to failure

jokes.text: 'cause you're a cook in the bedroom and a whore in the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he'll never do better than Liz
jokes.punchline: Backwards version of common saying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - gets common saying backwards but somehow makes it work

jokes.text: This is how everyone talked in the '80s. This is how Reagan talked.
jokes.setup: Present Jack questioning Past Jack's speech pattern
jokes.punchline: Claims it's authentic '80s speech like Reagan
jokes.explanation: Good period comedy with Reagan reference

jokes.text: This tuxedo is made out of the Puma that I rode into my 50th birthday party.
jokes.setup: Past Jack showing off his success
jokes.punchline: Tuxedo made from big cat he rode as transportation
jokes.explanation: Perfect alternate Jack excess - riding a puma and making clothes from it

jokes.text: It's after 6:00. What are we, farmers?
jokes.setup: Present Jack questioning the tuxedos
jokes.punchline: Classic Jack line about appropriate formal wear timing
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack callback that fans love - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: All pregnancies are hysterical. They're started by penises.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying all pregnancies are hysterical
jokes.punchline: Jenna's literal interpretation about penises causing hysteria
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna logic with wordplay on 'hysterical' meaning crazy vs. medical term

jokes.text: I cannot promise you that, Ken. I'm a horrible shot.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking Tracy to make shooting quick
jokes.punchline: Tracy admits he's bad at shooting so it will be worse
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Tracy being accidentally more threatening

jokes.text: It's like Netflix, but you go to a store, and you pick out your video from a limited selection. It'd be like five years ago.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing his new business idea
jokes.punchline: He's essentially describing Blockbuster as innovation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis moment - thinks he invented video stores, perfect character stupidity

jokes.text: All I have is this kick-ass laser pointer that I point at nerds' crotches in the park.
jokes.setup: Dennis not having a pen
jokes.punchline: Has laser pointer for harassing people in parks
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis cruelty with specific targeting of nerds

jokes.text: You have grandchildren and a gorgeous Polyblasian wife. That's a new race that emerges in 2030. They're incredible.
jokes.setup: Future Jack describing Jack's future happiness
jokes.punchline: Made-up future race with specific date and qualities
jokes.explanation: Good future comedy with absurd racial evolution concept

jokes.text: You'll spearhead GE's development of the electric vagina.
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack describing business success path
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous product development as corporate achievement
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate product that sounds like something GE would actually develop

jokes.text: In three years, they'll be married and living in Jacksonville, Florida, where he operates an unlicensed alligator park.
jokes.setup: Future Jack warning about Liz's future with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Specific prediction ending with illegal animal park
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis future - escalates from marriage to illegal dangerous business

jokes.text: We're obviously all thinking it, so I'm just gonna say it. We're gonna have sex with each other, right?
jokes.setup: Multiple Jacks together in room
jokes.punchline: Jack assumes they're all thinking about group sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack narcissism - assumes multiple versions of himself would have group sex

jokes.text: That wouldn't be very fair. I'm only trained to fight four or more men at a time.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening to fight Dennis
jokes.punchline: Admits his fighting training is for group combat only
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack moment - casual reference to specific elite combat training

jokes.text: I'm not gonna be held back by some uterus turd.
jokes.setup: Jenna discussing balancing career and motherhood
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude reference to potential baby
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna inappropriate language about motherhood

jokes.text: You'll still get laughed out of the Vanity Fair Oscar party by Greg Kinnear.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how TV diminishes movie careers
jokes.punchline: Specific actor as ultimate Hollywood gatekeeper
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific celebrity as measure of Hollywood status hierarchy
